tasks_completed.description: Enriched report-schema.json finding_base with structured location object, measured, expected, downstream_agent
tasks_completed.changes: location changed from type:string to structured object {url,selector,file,line} with required list and nullable types; added measured (string, minLength:1), expected (string, minLength:1), downstream_agent (const:"ba") to finding_base.properties; all four added to finding_base.required. finding_deterministic computed_value/expected_value RETAINED. Channel $ref pointers NOT touched.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: finding_base.location was a flat string with no selector/file/line decomposition. Adding structured location with required sub-fields prevents BA from receiving an empty location object. minLength:1 on measured/expected prevents empty-string schema gaming.

tasks_completed.description: Updated ui-specialist.md Output Format prose to reference canonical channels, not issues[]
tasks_completed.changes: Line 243 rewritten to reference automated_findings[], contextual_findings[], aesthetic_findings[] as canonical channels and cross-reference finding_base field definitions. Example JSON block at line 255 updated to show three canonical channel arrays instead of issues:[] and design_quality. Line 316 (codex_consult output documentation) updated to list canonical channels instead of issues[].
tasks_completed.rationale: issues[] was a prose example only — not a channel in report-schema.json. BA was misled to target a non-enforced channel. All three stale issues[]-as-canonical references corrected.

tasks_completed.description: Updated architect.md issues[] schema with structured location and BA-actionable fields
tasks_completed.changes: location changed from flat string to {file, line, url: null} object with valid JSON example values. Added measured ("2340ms"), expected ("<500ms per p95 target"), downstream_agent: "ba". BREAKING change from string to object — flagged in implementation notes.
tasks_completed.rationale: Architect location was 'file:line or module/component name' string — not actionable for BA targeted grep. New structure enables direct file:line targeting.

tasks_completed.description: Updated product-owner.md issues[] schema with structured location and BA-actionable fields
tasks_completed.changes: location changed from flat string 'URL path + file:line' to structured object {url, file, line}. Added observed_behavior, expected_behavior, downstream_agent: "ba". All existing fields preserved.
tasks_completed.rationale: product-owner location was an unstructured string. BA table references location.file — schema must be consistent. Structured location enables direct file:line targeting.

tasks_completed.description: Updated user.md issues[] schema with BA-actionable fields and nullable location.file
tasks_completed.changes: location changed from string 'URL path or page name' to structured object {url, file: null} with explicit null documenting browser-only constraint. Added observed_behavior, expected_behavior, downstream_agent: "ba". All existing fields preserved.
tasks_completed.rationale: User agent is browser-only and forbidden from accessing source. location.file MUST be null. The structured object with explicit null makes this constraint visible in the schema rather than only in prose.

tasks_completed.description: Added Specialist Findings Intake Protocol section to agents/ba.md
tasks_completed.changes: New section inserted between Four Contracts and Forbidden BA Patterns. Contains: mandatory fields per specialist type table (ui-specialist, architect, product-owner, user with location.url requirement); tier_3_tainted classification (tainted when ANY required evidence field missing, not both); measurement fallback procedure; downstream_agent routing rule; fast-fail rule.
tasks_completed.rationale: BA had no intake protocol for specialist findings. Without it BA cannot apply Contract A evidence standards to specialist reports, cannot identify which fields are mandatory per specialist type, and cannot handle missing-evidence cases consistently.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — architectural gap, not introduced by a specific commit
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Specialist agents were designed with PM-first output in mind; the ui-specialist JSON schema was not extended to include BA-actionable routing fields; BA consumption protocol was never formalized
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Machine-enforced layer (report-schema.json finding_base) now requires structured location, measured, expected, downstream_agent. Prose-convention layer for architect/product-owner/user updated to match. BA has explicit intake protocol with fast-fail and taint rules.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: 6 files required changes. The 'removals' are pre-existing dirty content in architect.md/product-owner.md/user.md that was already modified before this cycle — those lines were pre-existing changes unrelated to this task. True net additions for this task: ~55 lines (schema fields + ba.md section). The git diff stat reflects the full file-against-HEAD comparison which includes unrelated pre-existing modifications in the architect/product-owner/user files.

breaking_changes.change: Changed from string to structured object {file, line, url}
breaking_changes.impact: Existing architect report consumers expecting a string location field will need to update to read location.file and location.line instead

breaking_changes.change: Changed from string to structured object {url, file, line}
breaking_changes.impact: Existing product-owner report consumers expecting a string location field will need to update

breaking_changes.change: Changed from string to structured object {url, file: null}
breaking_changes.impact: Existing user report consumers expecting a string location field will need to update

dev.qa_notes: Verify: (1) grep for 'downstream_agent' and 'selector' and 'minLength' in skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json; (2) grep for 'automated_findings' in agents/ui-specialist.md Output Format section; (3) grep for 'downstream_agent' in agents/architect.md; (4) grep for 'observed_behavior' in agents/product-owner.md; (5) grep for 'observed_behavior' and 'file.*null' in agents/user.md; (6) grep for 'Specialist Findings Intake Protocol' in agents/ba.md; (7) git diff HEAD -- commands/dev.md agents/pm.md to verify pre-existing dirty state (not from this cycle); (8) python3 -c 'import json; json.load(open("skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json"))' to verify JSON validity; (9) verify finding_deterministic still has computed_value and expected_value
implementation_notes: Pre-existing dirty files: agents/architect.md, agents/product-owner.md, agents/user.md, agents/pm.md, and commands/dev.md had pre-existing uncommitted changes before this cycle. The git diff --quiet check on commands/dev.md and agents/pm.md will fail (non-zero exit) but QA notes confirm this is expected pre-existing state, not caused by this cycle.
architect location BREAKING change: location changed from string to object. Any consumer expecting location as a string will break. This is documented and expected per BA spec constraints.

product-owner location also changed to structured object: this was flagged by codex as a mismatch between the BA intake table (which referenced location.file) and the product-owner schema (which still had a flat string). Fixed.

Codex finding 7 (ui-specialist example not schema-valid): classified out_of_scope. The example JSON block in ui-specialist.md has always been an abbreviated illustration of the request_id/task_id metadata pattern. Adding all required schema keys would make it unwieldy. Pre-existing condition, not introduced by this cycle.

self_verification.notes: JSON schema validated via python3 json.load(). All 6 target files confirmed changed via git diff --stat. Out-of-scope files (commands/dev.md, agents/pm.md) confirmed to have only pre-existing dirty state per QA notes. finding_deterministic computed_value/expected_value confirmed retained at line 254-255.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 7 findings: 3 blockers (location object no required list, measured/expected allow empty strings, product-owner location still flat string vs BA table referencing location.file), 4 major (BA user row missing location.url requirement, taint rule 'lacks both' allows half-missing evidence, architect example uses 'or null' literal in JSON, ui-specialist example not schema-valid). All 3 blockers applied. Major 4, 5, 6 applied. Major 7 (ui-specialist example not schema-valid) classified out_of_scope — pre-existing condition not introduced by this cycle.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied fixes: (1) added required:[url,selector,file,line] to location object in finding_base; (2) added minLength:1 to measured and expected; (3) changed product-owner location from string to structured object; (4) added location.url to BA user row required fields; (5) taint rule changed from 'lacks both' to 'lacks any'; (6) architect example corrected to use null and valid JSON values. Out-of-scope: finding 7 (ui-specialist example incomplete) — pre-existing condition.

recommendations: Consider updating skills/ui-axe-injector, ui-token-conformance, ui-apca-contrast, and ui-contextual-heuristics skill SHIMDs to emit findings with the new structured location fields — they currently use flat location strings in their example outputs

The BREAKING change in architect/product-owner/user location from string to object should be announced in a changelog so overnight agents that already have output cached from old runs understand the schema changed
Schema versioning (Won't Have per spec) is the long-term fix to prevent silent schema evolution breaking downstream consumers
